title: Glechoma leucophylla
intro: Glechoma leucophylla, also known as Russian melissophyllum or Russian melissa, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to aid digestion, relieve anxiety and stress, and exhibit anti-inflammatory effects, contributing to improved overall health and well-being. The bioactive constituents of Glechoma leucophylla include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support digestive health and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Glechoma leucophylla is classified within the family Lamiaceae. This family is commonly known as the mint or deadnettle family. The genus Glechoma is a small group of flowering plants that are native to Europe and Asia. Glechoma leucophylla is also known as Russian sage, a perennial herb with a woody base and square stems. It is typically found in woodland edges, grasslands, and along streams in its native regions.
uses: Glechoma leucophylla has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported medicinal properties.
